title: Gironniera cuspidata
intro: Gironniera cuspidata, also known as Beluntas, is a plant species native to Southeast Asia, classified as a medicinal shrub or small tree in the family Moraceae. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever, diarrhea, and respiratory issues, due to its reported health benefits such as anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiviral properties. The bioactive constituents of Gironniera cuspidata include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Gironniera cuspidata is commonly prepared as a decoction or infusion, as well as being used to make herbal teas and tonics.
taxonomy: Gironniera cuspidata is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Salicaceae. It is a member of the order Malpighiales and the family Salicaceae, which includes willows and cottonwoods. The genus Gironniera is a group of tropical plants native to Southeast Asia. Gironniera cuspidata is a deciduous tree with distinctive leaves and is often found growing near water. The classification of Gironniera cuspidata is also supported by molecular phylogenetic studies that confirm its placement within the Salicaceae family.

side_effects: Gironniera cuspidata, also known as the water apple tree, is a plant native to Asia and has been used in traditional medicine for various purposes.
